user.title: Zero Net Export
user.description: Create the Zero Net Export backend entry first, then finish setup from Settings -> Devices & Services -> Integrations -> Zero Net Export -> Configure. Runtime defaults start in a safe bootstrap state until you map sources and add devices.

data_description.name: This is just the label Home Assistant will show for this Zero Net Export system. Pick something human-friendly, like the site, inverter, or switchboard name. You can finish source mapping, refresh tuning, and device onboarding after entry creation from Settings -> Devices & Services -> Integrations -> Zero Net Export -> Configure.

setup_incomplete.title: Finish native Zero Net Export setup

device_inventory_invalid.title: Managed-device configuration needs repair

runtime_attention.title: Zero Net Export runtime blockers

init.title: Zero Net Export command center

native_setup.title: Sensors

data_description.grid_sensor_mode: Choose Combined when your hardware exposes one signed/net grid sensor. Choose Separate when Home Assistant already has dedicated import and export entities.

native_setup_sources.title: Sensors

data.grid_energy_entity_manual: Combined / net grid energy entity ID fallback (optional)
data.battery_soc_entity_manual: Battery state of charge entity ID fallback (optional)

data_description.grid_power_entity: Single signed/net grid power sensor. Positive values are treated as import and negative values are treated as export.
data_description.grid_energy_entity: Single signed/net grid energy sensor. This uses a native dropdown instead of Home Assistant's entity picker so the combined-grid energy mapping can avoid the known entity/UUID validation bug on some installs. Positive values are treated as import and negative values are treated as export.
data_description.grid_energy_entity_manual: Optional native fallback if Home Assistant still rejects a valid Combined / net grid energy choice. Capture the exact error or a screenshot first, then clear the selector, paste the same energy entity ID here, and save again.
data_description.home_load_power_entity: Optional whole-home load power sensor. If omitted, Zero Net Export infers household load from solar, grid, and battery flows when possible.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Optional battery SOC sensor for reserve-aware control. This uses a native dropdown instead of Home Assistant's entity picker so battery reserve setup stays usable even on installs that hit the entity/UUID validation bug.
data_description.battery_soc_entity_manual: Optional native fallback if Home Assistant still rejects a valid Battery state of charge choice. Capture the exact error or a screenshot first, then clear the selector, paste the same battery SOC entity ID here, and save again.

devices.title: Managed Devices

data_description.device_action: Choose whether to promote a surfaced fixed-load or variable-load candidate when one fits, add a device manually when no surfaced candidate is right, open the Managed Devices workspace to toggle which devices stay enabled, edit an existing device, remove a device, or open the advanced JSON editor.

device_bulk_enable.title: Review managed devices workspace and enablement

device_edit_pick.title: Edit managed device

device_pick_candidate.title: Pick unmanaged candidate

device_pick_candidate_full.title: Full unmanaged candidate list

device_vetting.title: Review candidate for promotion

device_template.title: Choose managed-device preset

device_add.title: {device_mode} managed device

data_description.entity_id: Choose the Home Assistant entity that directly controls the real load. Use the manual Managed Devices form only when the shortlist or full surfaced list did not already surface the right entity, and review helpers carefully before saving.

device_remove.title: Remove managed device

devices_json.title: Managed Devices JSON editor

data_description.device_inventory_json: Advanced recovery path. Paste a JSON array of controllable devices here when you need bulk edits or to repair invalid managed-device configuration.

policy.title: Controls

data_description.target_export_w: The export value the controller aims for during normal operation. Leave this at 0 W unless you intentionally want a small export or import bias.
data_description.deadband_w: How much error Zero Net Export will tolerate before changing devices. Higher values reduce device flapping; lower values chase the target more aggressively.
data_description.battery_reserve_soc: The minimum battery percentage to protect before discretionary surplus-absorption actions are allowed. Choose the lowest battery level you still want to keep in reserve.
data_description.refresh_seconds: How often the backend re-reads your source sensors and re-plans control actions. Shorter intervals react faster; longer intervals reduce churn.

support.title: Diagnostics

advanced.title: Advanced JSON editor and recovery
advanced.description: Use the raw JSON editor only for advanced recovery or bulk managed-device repair, not for normal device onboarding.

error.device_inventory_invalid: The managed-device configuration is invalid or incomplete.
error.source_entities_invalid: One or more source entities are missing, duplicated, or invalid.
